40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/

6	module
7

9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing
11	
12	/-!
13	
14	# Basic properties of the scheme `Proj A`
15	
16	The scheme `Proj 𝒜` for a graded ring `𝒜` is constructed in
17	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean`.
18	In this file we provide basic properties of the scheme.
19	
20	## Main results
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.toSpecZero`: The structure map `Proj A ⟶ Spec (A 0)`.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec`:
23	  The canonical isomorphism `Proj A |_ D₊(f) ≅ Spec (A_f)₀`
24	  when `f` is homogeneous of positive degree.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι`: The open immersion `Spec (A_f)₀ ⟶ Proj A`.
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.affineOpenCover`: The open cover of `Proj A` by `Spec (A_f)₀` for all
27	  homogeneous `f` of positive degree.
28	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.stalkIso`:
29	  The stalk of `Proj A` at `x` is the degree `0` part of the localization of `A` at `x`.
30	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`:
31	  Given a map `f : A →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` such that the image of the irrelevant ideal under `f`
32	  generates the whole ring, we can construct a map `X ⟶ Proj 𝒜`.
33	
34	-/
35	
36	@[expose] public section
37	
38	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Proj
39	
40	open HomogeneousLocalization CategoryTheory
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
45	variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
46	variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
47	variable [GradedRing 𝒜]
48	
49	section basicOpen
50	
51	variable (f g : A)
52	
53	/-- The basic open set `D₊(f)` associated to `f : A`. -/
54	def basicOpen : (Proj 𝒜).Opens :=
55	  ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen 𝒜 f
56	
57	@[simp]
58	theorem mem_basicOpen (x : Proj 𝒜) :
59	    x ∈ basicOpen 𝒜 f ↔ f ∉ x.asHomogeneousIdeal :=
60	  Iff.rfl

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

/-- Project-local compat: `awayι = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen).fromSpec`. -/
lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = 
      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
  congr 1
  -- Goal: basicOpenIsoSpec.inv = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv
  have hcompat : (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom = 
                 (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.hom ≫ 
                 Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom := by
    rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom]
    rfl
  -- Apply Iso.hom_inv_id-style manipulation
  rw [← Iso.eq_inv_comp]
  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv]
  -- Goal: 𝟙 = (Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv) ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.hom
  rw [Category.assoc, hcompat]
  -- Goal: 𝟙 = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv ≫ isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.hom)
  rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, Spec.map_id]

end AlgebraicGeometry

  -- Goal: ((ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ map(eqToHom).op) ≫ Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv).app(...) = ...

lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]

example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) =
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
      (Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom 
        (Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).op := by
  change ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι).app _ = _
  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Opens.ι_app]
  -- Goal: presheaf.map(homOfLE_iimg).op ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app(ι ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(f)) = RHS
  -- ι ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(f) = ⊤
  -- basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app(⊤) = basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.appTop
  -- basicOpenIsoSpec = asIso basicOpenToSpec → inv = inverse
  -- Use the inv_appTop trick.
  
  -- We have basicOpenIsoSpec.hom = basicOpenToSpec, so basicOpenIsoSpec.inv is the inverse  
  -- of basicOpenToSpec. The appTop of basicOpenToSpec is:
  -- basicOpenToSpec_app_top : (basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f).app ⊤ = ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ awayToSection ≫ topIso.inv
  sorry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Proj.awayι` chart-evaluation infrastructure

The blueprint `chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` (iter-195/iter-196 analogist recipe
`lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot`, ANALOGUE_FOUND) identifies two project-local Mathlib lemmas
that, together, evaluate `((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤).inv` on the canonical generator
`isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 f`. The two lemmas mirror the Mathlib pattern
`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:560-564`)
applied to the `Proj`-side basic-open factorisation.

Each declaration below is project-local (general-purpose graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ`); none
mention `kbar` or `projectiveLineBarGrading`. They are placed here as supplements
because they have no upstream Mathlib home (yet). -/

/-- Project-local supplement: the preimage of `Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f` under
`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` is `⊤`. Combines `Proj.opensRange_awayι` with
`Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`. -/
lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self
    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]

/-- Project-local supplement: `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` factors as
`Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen ...).fromSpec`.

This is the "iter-195 analogist recipe step (i)" `Proj.awayι_isoSpec_compat` from
`analogies/lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot.md`. It is the bridge that lets us transport
Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (which is about `Γ(X, U)`, not the
project's `Away 𝒜 f`) into a closed-form for `(Proj.awayι).app`. -/
lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec
    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm =
      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
  congr 1
  -- Goal: `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv`.
  -- Repackage RHS as the `inv` of a single `Iso.trans` so we can apply `Iso.ext`
  -- on the `.hom` side, where the `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`/`basicOpenToSpec`/
  -- `isoSpec_hom = toSpecΓ` chain composes definitionally.
  rw [show (Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
       (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.inv) =
       ((Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec ≪≫
         Scheme.Spec.mapIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).op).inv from rfl]
  congr 1
  apply Iso.ext
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, Iso.trans_hom,
      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
  rfl

/-! ### Iter-193 Lane E refactor — `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq` route

The substantive content of `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` (the chart-1 `Proj.appIso`
evaluation `isLocElem ↦ 1`) repeatedly STUCK across iter-188/189/190/191 on the
`Proj.appIso` evaluation step. Iter-193 reroutes via `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq`:
since `iotaGm_r_1 = IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι X_1) onePt.left h_range` by
definition, *any* `Spec.map(F)` satisfying the same lift identity must equal

`kbarChart1Ring : Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* kbar`
sending `isLocElem ↦ 1` and prove `Spec.map kbarChart1Ring ≫ Proj.awayι X_1 =
onePt.left`, then `iotaGm_r_1 = Spec.map kbarChart1Ring` (by `lift_uniq`), and
the whole chart-1 evaluation reduces to a ring-map identity on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`. -/

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]

lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = 
      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
  congr 1
  rw [show (Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ 
       (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.inv) = 
       ((Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec ≪≫ 
         Scheme.Spec.mapIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).op).inv from rfl]
  congr 1
  apply Iso.ext
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, Iso.trans_hom, 
      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
  rfl

-- The conv-based approach should work for the dependent motive issue
example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) =
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
      (Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom 
        (Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).op := by
  have h_compat := Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm
  -- Convert the LHS using h_compat. The codomain type depends on awayι; rewrite at the
  -- function-only position via `rw [h_compat]` should work because Scheme.Hom.app is monomorphic.
  -- But the codomain of .app U has hidden `awayι ⁻¹ U` dependency, so we use simp_rw or conv.
  conv_lhs => rw [h_compat]  -- this might fail due to dependent type
  sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry

# AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean

## Summary

- **Added axiom-clean:** 2 declarations
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self` (line ~189)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec` (line ~199)
- **Blocked on:** `kbarChart1Ring_specMap_fac` (line 326) — still carries a `sorry` on
  the residual `appTop(Spec.map kbarChart1Ring) = appTop(iotaGm_r_1)` ring-map identity.
- **Blocked on:** `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` (line ~534) — same residual content modulo
  iso-chain functoriality.
- **Sorry count in this file**: 4 before → 4 after (no net delta in `sorry` count, but
  +2 axiom-clean reusable supplement lemmas at the Mathlib-graded-ring abstraction level).

## Session summary

The iter-196 Lane E directive was to close the two Lane E `sorry`s at lines 273 and 481
(now relocated to 326 and ~534 after the supplement insertion) via the blueprint-pinned
analogist recipe `lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot` (verdict ANALOGUE_FOUND, recipe in blueprint
`chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex:1265-1553`).

The blueprint specified three substrate lemmas to land:
1. `Proj.awayι_isoSpec_compat` — bridge `awayι` to `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec`.
2. `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` — section-level formula (port of `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self`).
3. `Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem` — point-value via `Iso.eq_inv_apply` + `awayToSection_apply`.

**This iteration landed (1) axiom-clean** as `Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec` and the
auxiliary `Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self`. **(2) and (3) did NOT close** due to
the dependent-motive issue described below.

## Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self (new, line ~189)
- **Approach:** Two-step rewrite: `← Proj.opensRange_awayι` then `Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. `lean_verify` confirms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec (new, line ~199)
- **Statement:** `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
  (isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec`.
- **Approach:** Unfold `Proj.awayι` and `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` to expose
  `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ ι` vs `Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv ≫ ι`. Cancel
  the trailing `ι` via `congr 1`. Repackage the RHS of the remaining iso equation as
  `(isoSpec ≪≫ Spec.mapIso basicOpenIsoAway.op).inv` so we can apply `Iso.ext` on the
  forward direction, where the chain
  `basicOpenIsoSpec.hom = basicOpenToSpec = toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map(awayToSection)`
  and `isoSpec.hom = toSpecΓ` makes the equation `rfl` after rewriting via
  `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`, `Proj.basicOpenToSpec`, `Iso.trans_hom`,
  `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom`, `Functor.mapIso_hom`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. `lean_verify` confirms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen (NOT ADDED)
- **Approach 1:** `conv_lhs => rw [Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec ...]` — FAILED.
  The `rw` motive is dependent: `Scheme.Hom.app f V` has codomain `Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ V)`, so
  changing `f` from `awayι` to `Spec.map(...) ≫ fromSpec` makes the type-checker fail
  because the codomain `awayι ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(f)` changes shape (even though both sides equal `⊤`).
- **Approach 2:** `congrArg (·.app (basicOpen 𝒜 f)) (Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec ...)` —
  FAILED with the same dependent-typing issue at the `congrArg` motive elaboration.
- **Approach 3:** Direct `change ((basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ ι).app _ = _)` to unfold `awayι`
  syntactically, then `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` to split. The first factor `ι.app(basicOpen 𝒜 f)`
  evaluates via `Scheme.Opens.ι_app` to `presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op`. The second factor
  `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app (ι ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen 𝒜 f)` needs to be evaluated; here `ι ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤`
  (by `Scheme.Opens.ι_preimage_self`), but `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app ⊤` requires
  inverting the `basicOpenToSpec.app ⊤` formula from `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top`.
  Concretely: need `Scheme.inv_app` + `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec`
  combined with `(asIso basicOpenToSpec).inv = inv basicOpenToSpec`. This last step requires
  careful handling of the iso construction. PARTIAL — substantive remainder ~5-15 LOC.
- **Next step:** Need an `appTop`-level identity. Specifically, build a helper
  `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.app ⊤ =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv`
  via `Scheme.inv_app` + `basicOpenToSpec_app_top` + the iso inversion. ~5-15 LOC.
  This is the missing piece. Plan for iter-197.

## Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem (NOT ADDED)
- **Approach:** Blueprint recipe step (ii) — `Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom` + `Iso.eq_inv_apply` +
  `awayToSection_apply`. Cannot be tried until `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` lands (it is
  the substrate primitive).
- **Result:** NOT ADDED.

## kbarChart1Ring_specMap_fac line 326 sorry (NOT CLOSED)
- **Approach 1:** Use the iter-194 structural reduction `rw [← iotaGm_r_1_fac]; congr 1; refine ext_of_isAffine` — already in the proof body, reducing the goal to
  `appTop(Spec.map(kbarChart1Ring)) = appTop(iotaGm_r_1)`. The residual sorry remains.
- **Approach 2:** Bypass `iotaGm_r_1` entirely by using `Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec`
  on the original goal `Spec.map(kbarChart1Ring) ≫ awayι X_1 = onePt.left`. This rewrites
  to `Spec.map(kbarChart1Ring) ≫ Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ fromSpec = onePt.left`,
  i.e., `Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ kbarChart1Ring) ≫ fromSpec = onePt.left`. This
  needs a characterization of `onePt.left` factoring through `fromSpec`. The natural
  characterization is `onePt.left = (something) ≫ fromSpec` via the universal property of
  `fromSpec` (as an open immersion). PARTIAL — substantive remainder still ~10-20 LOC.
- **Next step:** With `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` landed (per the iter-197 plan above),
  follow the iter-196 blueprint dispatch:
  1. Rewrite RHS using `Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem`.
  2. Use `ext_of_isAffine` + `awayToSection_apply` to identify both ring maps.
  3. Close via `kbarChart1Ring` definitional unfolding + `MvPolynomial.eval` collapse.

## iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval line ~534 sorry (NOT CLOSED)
- **Approach:** Same recipe as `kbarChart1Ring_specMap_fac` modulo the iso-chain
  functoriality stages 5–8 in the existing proof body. Pre-requisite: `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen`.
- **Result:** NOT ADDED.

## Why I stopped

**Real progress:** 2 axiom-clean declarations added.

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self` (line ~189) — supplement that
  combines `Proj.opensRange_awayι` with `Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`. General-purpose
  (graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ`); not project-specific.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec` (line ~199) — the bridge between
  `Proj.awayι` and `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` for the basic open `D₊(f)`. General-purpose
  (graded ring level); the substantive content of step (i) of the iter-195 analogist
  recipe `lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot`.

**Partial progress on Lane E HARD BAR:** Neither of the two Lane E `sorry`s closed.
The infrastructure I added is the FIRST of 3 prerequisite substrate primitives from the
blueprint recipe; the remaining 2 (`Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` + `Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem`)
are the load-bearing ones, but were blocked by the dependent-motive issue on
`Scheme.Hom.app` when trying to rewrite via the compat lemma.

**Specific blocker (named):** Proving `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` directly via the
compat lemma `Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec` fails because `Scheme.Hom.app f U` has
codomain `Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U)`, so rewriting `f` changes the codomain type. Mathlib has no
`congrArg`-style lemma for `Scheme.Hom.app` that handles this. Workaround: avoid the
rewrite path entirely, build `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top` as a fresh helper
(~5-15 LOC, mechanical via `Scheme.inv_app` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top`), then
chain through `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.ι_app`. This is the recommended
iter-197 path.

**Why not informal agent:** No `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` /
`OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` set in env (checked via `env | grep`). Informal agent
unavailable.

## Recommendations for iter-197 plan

1. **Continue Lane E with iter-197 prover dispatch.** The blueprint-pinned recipe still
   applies; the iter-196 supplements (`awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self`,
   `awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec`) are now in tree and reduce the remaining LOC budget by ~5.
2. **Highest-value next helper:** `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top` (~5-15 LOC).
   This unblocks `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` via `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` chase. The full
   chain `Proj.awayι → comp_app → ι_app + basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top` then gives the
   target section-level formula without the dependent-motive issue.
3. **Subsequent helper:** `Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem` (~5-10 LOC) by
   `Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app` + `awayToSection_apply` + `awayι_app_basicOpen`.
4. **Consumer dispatches** at line 326 and ~534 (~5-15 LOC each) drop into place.

**Total iter-197 LOC estimate (conditional on the iter-196 supplements landing):**
~25-45 LOC across 2 supplements + 2 consumer closures. Tight scope for a 1-iter dispatch.

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

-- Test: prove (basicOpenIsoSpec.inv).appTop = ...
example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  rw [Scheme.inv_appTop]
  -- Goal: inv (basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop) = topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  -- Goal: inv (ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ awayToSection ≫ topIso.inv) = topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv
  -- Need: awayToSection has IsIso (via basicOpenIsoAway)
  haveI : IsIso (Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f) := by
    rw [show Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from rfl]
    infer_instance
  rw [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.inv_comp]
  -- inv (topIso.inv) = topIso.hom, inv (ΓSpecIso.hom) = ΓSpecIso.inv
  congr 1
  · -- inv (topIso.inv) = topIso.hom
    exact Iso.eq_inv_comp _ |>.mp <| by simp
  · congr 1
    · -- inv (awayToSection) = basicOpenIsoAway.inv
      rfl
    · -- inv (ΓSpecIso.hom) = ΓSpecIso.inv
      rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

example {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] :
    have hh : IsIso (f.app ⊤) := inferInstance
    True := True.intro

example {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] :
    haveI : IsIso f.appTop := show IsIso (f.app ⊤) by infer_instance
    (inv f).appTop = inv f.appTop := Scheme.Hom.inv_appTop f

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  rw [show (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv = 
      inv (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from by simp]
  haveI hh : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
    show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
  rw [Scheme.Hom.inv_appTop]
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  haveI : IsIso (Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f) := by
    show IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom; infer_instance
  rw [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.inv_comp]
  -- inv (topIso.inv) ≫ inv (awayToSection) ≫ inv (ΓSpecIso.hom)
  -- = topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv
  congr 1
  · -- inv topIso.inv = topIso.hom
    simp
  congr 1
  · -- inv awayToSection = basicOpenIsoAway.inv (since basicOpenIsoAway.hom = awayToSection)
    rfl
  · -- inv ΓSpecIso.hom = ΓSpecIso.inv
    simp

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  -- Prove via Iso.eq_inv_comp: f = α.inv ≫ g ↔ α.hom ≫ f = g (or some variant)
  -- Specifically, multiply both sides by basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop on the left.
  -- LHS becomes basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.appTop, 
  --   then by Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop = (basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.hom).appTop
  --   = 𝟙.appTop = id.
  -- Wait, the composition is wrong. .inv.appTop is the appTop of the inv, which is a separate map.
  -- Actually Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop: (f ≫ g).appTop = g.appTop ≫ f.appTop... not directly useful.
  -- 
  -- Strategy: use Iso lemmas at the morphism (sheaf) level — basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app U is the 
  -- "inverse" of basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.app U.
  rw [show (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop = 
      inv (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop from by
        haveI : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
          show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
        rw [← Scheme.Hom.inv_appTop]
        congr 1
        simp]
  -- Now Goal: inv (basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop) = topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv
  haveI : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
    show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom]
  rw [Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  -- Goal: inv (ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ awayToSection ≫ topIso.inv) = topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv
  haveI : IsIso (Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f) := by
    show IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom; infer_instance
  rw [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.inv_comp]
  -- inv (topIso.inv) ≫ inv (awayToSection) ≫ inv (ΓSpecIso.hom)
  congr 1
  · simp
  congr 1
  · rfl
  · simp

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

example (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  -- Prove via Iso.eq_comp_inv: f = g ≫ α.inv ↔ f ≫ α.hom = g.
  -- We'll show: basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.appTop ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop = 𝟙
  -- and then β ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop = 𝟙 where β = RHS.
  -- This gives basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.appTop = β by mono cancellation.
  rw [← cancel_mono (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop]
  rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
  rw [Iso.inv_hom_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop]
  -- Goal: 𝟙 = (topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv) ≫ basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  -- Goal: 𝟙 = (topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ ΓSpecIso.inv) ≫ 
  --              (ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ awayToSection ≫ topIso.inv)
  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  -- Continue collapsing: basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ awayToSection = ?
  rw [show Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from rfl]
  rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  exact (Iso.hom_inv_id _).symm

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

lemma Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_appTop (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  haveI hh : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
    show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
  rw [← cancel_mono (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop,
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, Iso.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop]
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom]
  show 𝟙 _ = _ ≫ (Proj.basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f).app ⊤
  rw [Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  rw [show Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from rfl]
  -- Now combine everything with assoc and inv-hom collapses.
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id]

end AlgebraicGeometry

lemma Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_appTop (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  haveI hh : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
    show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
  rw [← cancel_mono (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop,
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, Iso.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop]
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom]
  show 𝟙 _ = _ ≫ (Proj.basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f).app ⊤
  rw [Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  rw [show Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from rfl]
  simp [← Functor.map_comp]

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u
open CategoryTheory

variable {σ : Type*} {A : Type u}
variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
variable [GradedRing 𝒜]

lemma Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_appTop (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.appTop =
      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
  haveI hh : IsIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop := 
    show IsIso ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.app ⊤) by infer_instance
  rw [← cancel_mono (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom.appTop,
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, Iso.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop]
  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom]
  show 𝟙 _ = _ ≫ (Proj.basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f).app ⊤
  rw [Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top]
  rw [show Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom from rfl]
  simp
  -- Goal at this point: 𝟙 = presheaf.map(eqToHom).op ≫ presheaf.map(eqToHom).op  
  -- (after topIso.hom and topIso.inv have been merged into presheaf.map(eqToHom) via simp)
  -- Use simp with eqToHom-related lemmas
  simp [Scheme.Opens.topIso, ← Functor.map_comp]

end AlgebraicGeometry

  and note in your task_results that the plan agent should flesh it out.
Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.


## Developer feedback channel (optional)

If during this iteration you notice something that would make Archon
better — a missing capability, redundant functionality, a
prompt instruction that contradicts itself, a tool you wish existed,
new ideas for better efficiency, etc — you may leave a short note
for the developer by appending to this file with a bash heredoc:

    cat >> /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/.debug-feedback/debug_feedback.md <<'EOF'

Rules:

- Only leave a note if you have something concrete to say. Empty or
  generic feedback ("everything went fine") is noise — skip it.
- One concrete observation per note. Keep it under ~200 words.
- This is optional and does not affect your task. Skip it if nothing

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry
157	
158	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
159	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
160	acceptable for iter-165). -/
161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
210	        have := hp
211	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
212	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
213	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
214	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
215	        exact this
216	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
217	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
218	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
219	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
220	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
221	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
222	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
223	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
224	        by_contra h
225	        push Not at h
226	        apply hd_ne
227	        ext k
228	        fin_cases k
229	        · simpa using h.1
230	        · simpa using h.2
231	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
232	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
233	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
234	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
235	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
236	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
237	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
238	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
239	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
240	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
241	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
242	
243	end AlgebraicGeometry
244	
245	end
