636	\section{Out of scope}
637	
638	The following items lie outside this chapter and are scheduled into
639	sibling sub-builds:
640	\begin{itemize}
641	  \item \emph{General coherent-sheaf cohomology vanishing.} Higher
642	    cohomology vanishing for general coherent sheaves on a curve
643	    requires Grothendieck vanishing on a one-dimensional scheme
644	    (Hartshorne~III.2.7), which the chapter does \emph{not} use; the
645	    chapter is narrowly tailored to the flasque/skyscraper case
646	    \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\) that the genus-\(0\) critical path needs.
647	  \item \emph{Serre duality for curves.} The general-\(g\) Riemann--Roch
648	    statement \(\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) routes through
649	    Serre duality \(H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes
650	    \mathcal L^{-1})^*\); Mathlib has neither the dualizing sheaf
651	    \(\omega_C\) on a scheme nor the duality theorem. The chapter
652	    sidesteps duality entirely by using flasqueness directly (the
653	    Hartshorne~III~\S2 order of presentation, not the
654	    Hartshorne~III~\S7 one).
655	  \item \emph{The \(H^0\) half of \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\).} The
656	    one-dimensionality identification \(\dim H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) is the
657	    sibling substrate lemma
658	    REF of
659	    \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}, closed axiom-clean iter-188
660	    against the project's four-step \(\bar k\)-linear-equivalence chain.
661	    The present chapter is the complementary H\(^1\) half.
662	\end{itemize}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2023 Dagur Asgeirsson. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Dagur Asgeirsson
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.DenseSubsite.SheafEquiv
10	/-!
11	
12	# The constant sheaf
13	
14	We define the constant sheaf functor (the sheafification of the constant presheaf)
15	`constantSheaf : D ⥤ Sheaf J D` and prove that it is left adjoint to evaluation at a terminal
16	object (see `constantSheafAdj`).
17	
18	We also define a predicate on sheaves, `Sheaf.IsConstant`, saying that a sheaf is in the
19	essential image of the constant sheaf functor.
20	
21	## Main results
22	
23	* `Sheaf.isConstant_iff_isIso_counit_app`: Provided that the constant sheaf functor is fully
24	  faithful, a sheaf is constant if and only if the counit of the constant sheaf adjunction applied
25	  to it is an isomorphism.
26	
27	* `Sheaf.isConstant_iff_of_equivalence` : The property of a sheaf of being constant is invariant
28	  under equivalence of sheaf categories.
29	
30	* `Sheaf.isConstant_iff_forget` : Given a "forgetful" functor `U : D ⥤ B` a sheaf `F : Sheaf J D` is
31	  constant if and only if the sheaf given by postcomposition with `U` is constant.
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	namespace CategoryTheory
37	
38	open Limits Opposite Category Functor Sheaf Adjunction
39	
40	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
41	variable (D : Type*) [Category* D]
42	
43	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
44	/-- The constant presheaf functor is left adjoint to evaluation at a terminal object. -/
45	@[simps! unit_app counit_app_app]
46	noncomputable def constantPresheafAdj {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
47	    Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⊣ (evaluation Cᵒᵖ D).obj (op T) where
48	  unit := (Functor.constCompEvaluationObj D (op T)).hom
49	  counit := {
50	    app := fun F => {
51	      app := fun ⟨X⟩ => F.map (IsTerminal.from hT X).op
52	      naturality := fun _ _ _ => by
53	        simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Functor.const_obj_obj, Functor.id_obj, Functor.const_obj_map,
54	          Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
55	        congr
56	        simp }
57	    naturality := by intros; ext; simp /- Note: `aesop` works but is kind of slow -/ }
58	
59	variable [HasWeakSheafify J D]
60	
61	/--
62	The functor which maps an object of `D` to the constant sheaf at that object, i.e. the
63	sheafification of the constant presheaf.
64	-/
65	noncomputable def constantSheaf : D ⥤ Sheaf J D := Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ (presheafToSheaf J D)
66	
67	/-- The constant sheaf functor is left adjoint to evaluation at a terminal object. -/
68	@[simps! counit_app]
69	noncomputable def constantSheafAdj {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
70	    constantSheaf J D ⊣ (sheafSections J D).obj (op T) :=
71	  (constantPresheafAdj D hT).comp (sheafificationAdjunction J D)
72	
73	variable {D}
74	
75	namespace Sheaf
76	
77	/--
78	A sheaf is constant if it is in the essential image of the constant sheaf functor.
79	-/
80	class IsConstant (F : Sheaf J D) : Prop where
81	  mem_essImage : (constantSheaf J D).essImage F
82	
83	lemma mem_essImage_of_isConstant (F : Sheaf J D) [IsConstant J F] :
84	    (constantSheaf J D).essImage F :=
85	  IsConstant.mem_essImage
86	
87	lemma isConstant_congr {F G : Sheaf J D} (i : F ≅ G) [IsConstant J F] : IsConstant J G where
88	  mem_essImage := essImage.ofIso i F.mem_essImage_of_isConstant
89	
90	lemma isConstant_of_iso {F : Sheaf J D} {X : D} (i : F ≅ (constantSheaf J D).obj X) :
91	    IsConstant J F := ⟨_, ⟨i.symm⟩⟩
92	
93	lemma isConstant_iff_mem_essImage {L : D ⥤ Sheaf J D} {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
94	    (adj : L ⊣ (sheafSections J D).obj ⟨T⟩)
95	    (F : Sheaf J D) : IsConstant J F ↔ L.essImage F := by
96	  rw [essImage_eq_of_natIso (adj.leftAdjointUniq (constantSheafAdj J D hT))]
97	  exact ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
98	
99	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
100	lemma isConstant_of_isIso_counit_app (F : Sheaf J D) [HasTerminal C]

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Sites.Fpqc
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.Shapes.Terminal
10	
11	/-!
12	# Sheaf of continuous maps associated to topological space
13	
14	Given a topological space `T`, we consider the presheaf on `Scheme` given by `U ↦ C(U, T)`
15	and show that it is a Zariski sheaf (TODO: show that it is a fpqc sheaf).
16	When `T` is discrete, this is the constant sheaf associated to `T` (TODO).
17	
18	## Main declarations
19	
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.continuousMapPresheaf`: The sheaf `U ↦ C(U, T)` for a topological space `T`.
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.continuousMapPresheafAb`: For a topological abelian group `A`, this is
22	  `continuousMapPresheaf A` viewed as a sheaf of abelian groups.
23	
24	## TODOs
25	
26	- Show that `continuousMapPresheaf` is a sheaf for the fpqc topology (@chrisflav).
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits
32	
33	universe w' w v₂ u₂ v u
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	variable (S : Scheme.{u}) (T : Type v) [TopologicalSpace T]
38	
39	/--
40	The yoneda embedding of `TopCat` precomposed with the forgetful functor from `Scheme`. This is the
41	presheaf `U ↦ C(U, T)`. For universe reasons, we implement it by hand.
42	-/
43	@[simps]
44	def continuousMapPresheaf (T : Type v) [TopologicalSpace T] : Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max v u) where
45	  obj U := C(U.unop, T)
46	  map {U V} f := TypeCat.ofHom fun g ↦ ContinuousMap.comp g f.unop.base.hom
47	
48	/-- `continuousMapPresheaf` is isomorphic to the composition of the forgetful
49	functor to `TopCat` and the yoneda embedding. -/
50	def continuousMapPresheafIsoUlift :
51	    continuousMapPresheaf T ≅
52	      Scheme.forgetToTop.op ⋙ TopCat.uliftFunctor.op ⋙ yoneda.obj (.of <| ULift T) :=
53	  NatIso.ofComponents fun U ↦ equivEquivIso <|
54	    (ContinuousMap.uliftEquiv U.1 T).symm.trans
55	    (TopCat.Hom.equivContinuousMap
56	      (TopCat.uliftFunctor.obj <| Scheme.forgetToTop.obj U.1)
57	      (TopCat.uliftFunctor.obj (TopCat.of T))).symm
58	
59	lemma isSheaf_zariskiTopology_continuousMapPresheaf :
60	    Presheaf.IsSheaf Scheme.zariskiTopology (continuousMapPresheaf T) := by
61	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_of_iso_iff (continuousMapPresheafIsoUlift T)]
62	  apply Scheme.forgetToTop.op_comp_isSheaf_of_isSheaf _ TopCat.grothendieckTopology
63	  apply TopCat.uliftFunctor.op_comp_isSheaf_of_isSheaf _ TopCat.grothendieckTopology
64	  rw [isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
65	  exact GrothendieckTopology.Subcanonical.isSheaf_of_isRepresentable _
66	
67	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
68	lemma isSheaf_fpqcTopology_continuousMapPresheaf :
69	    Presheaf.IsSheaf Scheme.fpqcTopology (continuousMapPresheaf T) := by
70	  rw [isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type, Scheme.fpqcTopology_eq_propQCTopology,
71	    isSheaf_type_propQCTopology_iff]
72	  refine ⟨?_, fun {R S} f hf₁ hf₂ ↦ ?_⟩
73	  · rw [← isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
74	    exact isSheaf_zariskiTopology_continuousMapPresheaf _
75	  · rw [Presieve.isSheafFor_singleton]
76	    have : Topology.IsQuotientMap (Spec.map f) := Flat.isQuotientMap_of_surjective _
77	    intro (x : C(Spec S, T)) h
78	    refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
79	    · refine Topology.IsQuotientMap.lift this x fun a b hfab ↦ ?_
80	      obtain ⟨c, rfl, rfl⟩ := Scheme.Pullback.exists_preimage_pullback a b hfab
81	      exact congr($(h (pullback.fst (Spec.map f) (Spec.map f))
82	        (pullback.snd _ _) pullback.condition).1 c)
83	    · apply Topology.IsQuotientMap.lift_comp
84	    · intro y hy
85	      rwa [← ContinuousMap.cancel_right (Spec.map f).surjective, Topology.IsQuotientMap.lift_comp]
86	
87	/-- `continuousMapPresheaf` is `U ↦ C(ConnectedComponents U, T)` if `T` is totally
88	disconnected. -/
89	def continuousMapPresheafEquivOfTotallyDisconnectedSpace [TotallyDisconnectedSpace T]
90	    (U : Scheme.{u}) :
91	    (continuousMapPresheaf T).obj (.op U) ≃ C(ConnectedComponents U, T) where
92	  toFun f := ⟨f.continuous.connectedComponentsLift, f.continuous.connectedComponentsLift_continuous⟩
93	  invFun f := .comp f ⟨ConnectedComponents.mk, ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe⟩
94	  right_inv f := by
95	    apply ContinuousMap.coe_injective
96	    dsimp
97	    exact (Continuous.connectedComponentsLift_unique _ _ (by simp)).symm
98	
99	/-- `continuousMapPresheaf` as a presheaf of abelian groups associated to a topological abelian
100	group. -/
101	def continuousMapPresheafAb (A : Type v) [TopologicalSpace A] [AddCommGroup A]
102	    [IsTopologicalAddGroup A] :
103	    Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{max v u} where
104	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of C(U.unop, A)
105	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (ContinuousMap.compAddMonoidHom' f.unop.base.hom)
106	
107	variable (A : Type v) [TopologicalSpace A] [AddCommGroup A] [IsTopologicalAddGroup A]
108	
109	/-- `continuousMapPresheafAb` viewed as a type valued sheaf is isomorphic to
110	`continuousMapPresheaf. -/
111	def continuousMapPresheafAbForgetIso :
112	    continuousMapPresheafAb A ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget Ab ≅ continuousMapPresheaf A :=
113	  Iso.refl _
114	
115	lemma isSheaf_fpqcTopology_continuousMapPresheafAb :
116	    Presheaf.IsSheaf Scheme.fpqcTopology (continuousMapPresheafAb A) := by
117	  apply Presheaf.isSheaf_of_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget Ab)
118	  exact isSheaf_fpqcTopology_continuousMapPresheaf _
119	
120	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Brian Nugent. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Brian Nugent
5	-/
6	
7	module
8	
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.EpiMono
10	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.AddCommGrpCat
11	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.LocallySurjective
12	
13	/-!
14	# Flasque Sheaves
15	
16	We define and prove basic properties about flasque sheaves on topological spaces.
17	
18	## Main definition
19	
20	* `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque`: A sheaf is flasque if all of the restriction morphisms are epimorphisms.
21	
22	## Main results
23	
24	* `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact`: Given a short exact sequence of sheaves,
25	  `0 ⟶ 𝓕 ⟶ 𝓖 ⟶ 𝓗 ⟶ 0`, if `𝓕` is flasque then `𝓖(U) ⟶ 𝓗(U)` is surjective, for any open `U`.
26	
27	* `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.of_shortExact_of_isFlasque₁₂ `: Given a short exact sequence of
28	  sheaves, `0 ⟶ 𝓕 ⟶ 𝓖 ⟶ 𝓗 ⟶ 0`, if `𝓕` and `𝓖` are flasque, then `𝓗` is flasque.
29	
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public section
33	
34	universe u v w
35	
36	open TopCat TopologicalSpace Opposite CategoryTheory Presheaf Limits
37	open scoped AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {X : TopCat.{u}}
40	
41	namespace TopCat
42	
43	namespace Presheaf
44	
45	variable {C : Type v} [Category.{w} C] (F : Presheaf C X)
46	
47	/-- A sheaf is flasque if all of the restriction morphisms are epimorphisms. -/
48	class IsFlasque : Prop where
49	  epi : ∀ {U V : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (i : U ⟶ V), Epi (F.map i)
50	
51	namespace IsFlasque
52	
53	attribute [instance low] IsFlasque.epi
54	
55	instance pushforward_isFlasque {Y : TopCat.{u}} [IsFlasque F] (f : X ⟶ Y) :
56	    IsFlasque (f _* F) where
57	  epi {U V} i := by
58	    simp only [pushforward_obj_obj, pushforward_obj_map]
59	    infer_instance
60	
61	end IsFlasque
62	
63	end Presheaf
64	
65	namespace Sheaf
66	
67	/-- A sheaf is flasque if it is flasque as a presheaf -/
68	abbrev IsFlasque {C : Type v} [Category.{w} C] (F : Sheaf C X) := Presheaf.IsFlasque F.obj
69	
70	namespace IsFlasque
71	
72	instance pushforward_isFlasque {C : Type v} [Category.{w} C] {Y : TopCat.{u}} (F : Sheaf C X)
73	    [IsFlasque F] (f : X ⟶ Y) : IsFlasque ((pushforward C f).obj F) :=
74	  Presheaf.IsFlasque.pushforward_isFlasque F.1 f

12	/-!
13	# Skyscraper (pre)sheaves
14	
15	A skyscraper (pre)sheaf `𝓕 : (Pre)Sheaf C X` is the (pre)sheaf with value `A` at point `p₀` that is
16	supported only at open sets contain `p₀`, i.e. `𝓕(U) = A` if `p₀ ∈ U` and `𝓕(U) = *` if `p₀ ∉ U`
17	where `*` is a terminal object of `C`. In terms of stalks, `𝓕` is supported at all specializations
18	of `p₀`, i.e. if `p₀ ⤳ x` then `𝓕ₓ ≅ A` and if `¬ p₀ ⤳ x` then `𝓕ₓ ≅ *`.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	* `skyscraperPresheaf`: `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` is the skyscraper presheaf at point `p₀` with
23	  value `A`.
24	* `skyscraperSheaf`: the skyscraper presheaf satisfies the sheaf condition.
25	
26	## Main statements
27	
28	* `skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes`: if `y ∈ closure {p₀}` then the stalk of
29	  `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is `A`.
30	* `skyscraperPresheafStalkOfNotSpecializes`: if `y ∉ closure {p₀}` then the stalk of
31	  `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is `*` the terminal object.
32	
33	TODO: generalize universe level when calculating stalks, after generalizing universe level of stalk.
34	TODO(@joelriou): refactor the definitions in this file so as to make them
35	particular cases of general constructions for points of sites from
36	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/Skyscraper.lean`.
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	noncomputable section
43	
44	open TopologicalSpace TopCat CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Opposite
45	open scoped AlgebraicGeometry
46	
47	universe u v w
48	
49	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} (p₀ : X) [∀ U : Opens X, Decidable (p₀ ∈ U)]
50	
51	section
52	
53	variable {C : Type v} [Category.{w} C] [HasTerminal C] (A : C)
54	
55	/-- A skyscraper presheaf is a presheaf supported at a single point: if `p₀ ∈ X` is a specified
56	point, then the skyscraper presheaf `𝓕` with value `A` is defined by `U ↦ A` if `p₀ ∈ U` and
57	`U ↦ *` if `p₀ ∉ A` where `*` is some terminal object.
58	-/
59	@[simps]
60	def skyscraperPresheaf : Presheaf C X where
61	  obj U := if p₀ ∈ unop U then A else terminal C
62	  map {U V} i :=
63	    if h : p₀ ∈ unop V then eqToHom <| by rw [if_pos h, if_pos (by simpa using i.unop.le h)]
64	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _
65	  map_id U :=
66	    (em (p₀ ∈ U.unop)).elim (fun h => dif_pos h) fun h =>
67	      ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext _ _
68	  map_comp {U V W} iVU iWV := by
69	    by_cases hW : p₀ ∈ unop W
70	    · have hV : p₀ ∈ unop V := leOfHom iWV.unop hW
71	      simp only [dif_pos hW, dif_pos hV, eqToHom_trans]
72	    · dsimp; rw [dif_neg hW]; apply ((if_neg hW).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
73	
74	theorem skyscraperPresheaf_eq_pushforward
75	    [hd : ∀ U : Opens (TopCat.of PUnit.{u + 1}), Decidable (PUnit.unit ∈ U)] :
76	    skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A =
77	      (ofHom (ContinuousMap.const (TopCat.of PUnit) p₀)) _*
78	        skyscraperPresheaf (X := TopCat.of PUnit) PUnit.unit A := by
79	  convert_to @skyscraperPresheaf X p₀ (fun U => hd <| (Opens.map <| ofHom <|
80	      ContinuousMap.const _ p₀).obj U)
81	    C _ _ A = _ <;> congr
82	
83	/-- Taking skyscraper presheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by
84	sending every `f : a ⟶ b` to the natural transformation `α` defined as: `α(U) = f : a ⟶ b` if
85	`p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object in `C` if `p₀ ∉ U`.
86	-/
87	@[simps]
88	def SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' {a b : C} (f : a ⟶ b) :
89	    skyscraperPresheaf p₀ a ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ b where
90	  app U :=
91	    if h : p₀ ∈ U.unop then eqToHom (if_pos h) ≫ f ≫ eqToHom (if_pos h).symm
92	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _
93	  naturality U V i := by
94	    simp only [skyscraperPresheaf_map]
95	    by_cases hV : p₀ ∈ V.unop
96	    · have hU : p₀ ∈ U.unop := leOfHom i.unop hV
97	      simp only [skyscraperPresheaf_obj, hU, hV, ↓reduceDIte, eqToHom_trans_assoc, Category.assoc,
98	        eqToHom_trans]
99	    · apply ((if_neg hV).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
100	
101	theorem SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_id {a : C} :
102	    SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ (𝟙 a) = 𝟙 _ := by
103	  ext U
104	  simp only [SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_app]; split_ifs <;> cat_disch
105	
106	theorem SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_comp {a b c : C} (f : a ⟶ b) (g : b ⟶ c) :
107	    SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ (f ≫ g) =
108	      SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ f ≫ SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ g := by
109	  ext U
110	  simp only [SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_app]
111	  split_ifs with h <;> cat_disch
112	
113	/-- Taking skyscraper presheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by
114	sending every `f : a ⟶ b` to the natural transformation `α` defined as: `α(U) = f : a ⟶ b` if
115	`p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object in `C` if `p₀ ∉ U`.
116	-/
117	@[simps]
118	def skyscraperPresheafFunctor : C ⥤ Presheaf C X where
119	  obj := skyscraperPresheaf p₀
120	  map := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀
121	  map_id _ := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_id p₀
122	  map_comp := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_comp p₀
123	
124	end
125	
126	section
127	
128	-- In this section, we calculate the stalks for skyscraper presheaves.
129	-- We need to restrict universe level.
130	variable {C : Type v} [Category.{u} C] (A : C) [HasTerminal C]
131	
132	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
133	/-- The cocone at `A` for the stalk functor of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` when `y ∈ closure {p₀}`
134	-/
135	@[simps]
136	def skyscraperPresheafCoconeOfSpecializes {y : X} (h : p₀ ⤳ y) :
137	    Cocone ((OpenNhds.inclusion y).op ⋙ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A) where
138	  pt := A
139	  ι :=
140	    { app := fun U => eqToHom <| if_pos <| h.mem_open U.unop.1.2 U.unop.2
141	      naturality := fun U V inc => by
142	        change dite _ _ _ ≫ _ = _; rw [dif_pos]
143	        swap
144	        · exact h.mem_open V.unop.1.2 V.unop.2
145	        · simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, skyscraperPresheaf_obj, unop_op,
146	            Functor.const_obj_obj, eqToHom_trans, Functor.const_obj_map, Category.comp_id] }
147	
148	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
149	/--
150	The cocone at `A` for the stalk functor of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` when `y ∈ closure {p₀}` is a
151	colimit
152	-/
153	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafCoconeIsColimitOfSpecializes {y : X} (h : p₀ ⤳ y) :
154	    IsColimit (skyscraperPresheafCoconeOfSpecializes p₀ A h) where
155	  desc c := eqToHom (if_pos trivial).symm ≫ c.ι.app (op ⊤)
156	  fac c U := by
157	    dsimp
158	    rw [← c.w (homOfLE <| (le_top : unop U ≤ _)).op]
159	    change _ ≫ _ ≫ dite _ _ _ ≫ _ = _
160	    rw [dif_pos]
161	    · simp only [eqToHom_trans_assoc,
162	        eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp, op_unop]
163	    · exact h.mem_open U.unop.1.2 U.unop.2
164	  uniq c f h := by
165	    dsimp
166	    rw [← h, skyscraperPresheafCoconeOfSpecializes_ι_app, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl,
167	      Category.id_comp]
168	
169	/-- If `y ∈ closure {p₀}`, then the stalk of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is `A`.
170	-/
171	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes [HasColimits C] {y : X} (h : p₀ ⤳ y) :
172	    (skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A).stalk y ≅ A :=
173	  colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, skyscraperPresheafCoconeIsColimitOfSpecializes p₀ A h⟩
174	
175	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
176	lemma germ_skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes_hom [HasColimits C] {y : X} (h : p₀ ⤳ y) (U hU) :
177	    (skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A).germ U y hU ≫
178	      (skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes p₀ A h).hom = eqToHom (if_pos (h.mem_open U.2 hU)) :=
179	  colimit.isoColimitCocone_ι_hom _ _
180	
181	/-- The cocone at `*` for the stalk functor of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` when `y ∉ closure {p₀}`
182	-/
183	@[simps]
184	def skyscraperPresheafCocone (y : X) :
185	    Cocone ((OpenNhds.inclusion y).op ⋙ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A) where
186	  pt := terminal C
187	  ι :=
188	    { app := fun _ => terminal.from _
189	      naturality := fun _ _ _ => terminalIsTerminal.hom_ext _ _ }
190	
191	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
192	/--
193	The cocone at `*` for the stalk functor of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` when `y ∉ closure {p₀}` is a
194	colimit
195	-/
196	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafCoconeIsColimitOfNotSpecializes {y : X} (h : ¬p₀ ⤳ y) :
197	    IsColimit (skyscraperPresheafCocone p₀ A y) :=
198	  let h1 : ∃ U : OpenNhds y, p₀ ∉ U.1 :=
199	    let ⟨U, ho, h₀, hy⟩ := not_specializes_iff_exists_open.mp h
200	    ⟨⟨⟨U, ho⟩, h₀⟩, hy⟩
201	  { desc := fun c => eqToHom (if_neg h1.choose_spec).symm ≫ c.ι.app (op h1.choose)
202	    fac := fun c U => by
203	      change _ = c.ι.app (op U.unop)
204	      simp only [← c.w (homOfLE <| @inf_le_left _ _ h1.choose U.unop).op, ←
205	        c.w (homOfLE <| @inf_le_right _ _ h1.choose U.unop).op, ← Category.assoc]
206	      congr 1
207	      refine ((if_neg ?_).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext _ _
208	      exact fun h => h1.choose_spec h.1
209	    uniq := fun c f H => by
210	      dsimp
211	      rw [← Category.id_comp f, ← H, ← Category.assoc]
212	      congr 1; apply terminalIsTerminal.hom_ext }
213	
214	/-- If `y ∉ closure {p₀}`, then the stalk of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is isomorphic to a
215	terminal object.
216	-/
217	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafStalkOfNotSpecializes [HasColimits C] {y : X} (h : ¬p₀ ⤳ y) :
218	    (skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A).stalk y ≅ terminal C :=
219	  colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, skyscraperPresheafCoconeIsColimitOfNotSpecializes _ A h⟩
220	
221	/-- If `y ∉ closure {p₀}`, then the stalk of `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is a terminal object
222	-/
223	def skyscraperPresheafStalkOfNotSpecializesIsTerminal [HasColimits C] {y : X} (h : ¬p₀ ⤳ y) :
224	    IsTerminal ((skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A).stalk y) :=
225	  IsTerminal.ofIso terminalIsTerminal <| (skyscraperPresheafStalkOfNotSpecializes _ _ h).symm
226	
227	theorem skyscraperPresheaf_isSheaf : (skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A).IsSheaf := by
228	  classical exact
229	    (Presheaf.isSheaf_iso_iff (eqToIso <| skyscraperPresheaf_eq_pushforward p₀ A)).mpr <|
230	      (Sheaf.pushforward_sheaf_of_sheaf _
231	        (Presheaf.isSheaf_on_punit_of_isTerminal _ (by
232	          dsimp [skyscraperPresheaf]
233	          rw [if_neg]
234	          · exact terminalIsTerminal
235	          · #adaptation_note /-- 2024-03-24
236	            Previously the universe annotation was not needed here. -/
237	            exact Set.notMem_empty PUnit.unit.{u + 1})))
238	
239	/--
240	The skyscraper presheaf supported at `p₀` with value `A` is the sheaf that assigns `A` to all opens
241	`U` that contain `p₀` and assigns `*` otherwise.
242	-/
243	def skyscraperSheaf : Sheaf C X :=
244	  ⟨skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A, skyscraperPresheaf_isSheaf _ _⟩
245	
246	/-- Taking skyscraper sheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by
247	sending every `f : a ⟶ b` to the natural transformation `α` defined as: `α(U) = f : a ⟶ b` if
248	`p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object in `C` if `p₀ ∉ U`.
249	-/
250	def skyscraperSheafFunctor : C ⥤ Sheaf C X where
251	  obj c := skyscraperSheaf p₀ c
252	  map f := ObjectProperty.homMk <| (skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀).map f
253	  map_id _ := Sheaf.hom_ext <| (skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀).map_id _
254	  map_comp _ _ := Sheaf.hom_ext <| (skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀).map_comp _ _
255	
256	namespace StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs
257	
258	variable [HasColimits C]
259	
260	/-- If `f : 𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c`, then a natural transformation `𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c` can be
261	defined by: `𝓕.germ p₀ ≫ f : 𝓕(U) ⟶ c` if `p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object
262	if `p₀ ∉ U`.
263	-/
264	@[simps]
265	def toSkyscraperPresheaf {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c) :
266	    𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c where
267	  app U :=
268	    if h : p₀ ∈ U.unop then 𝓕.germ _ p₀ h ≫ f ≫ eqToHom (if_pos h).symm
269	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _
270	  naturality U V inc := by
271	    dsimp
272	    by_cases hV : p₀ ∈ V.unop
273	    · have hU : p₀ ∈ U.unop := leOfHom inc.unop hV
274	      split_ifs
275	      rw [← Category.assoc, 𝓕.germ_res' inc, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, eqToHom_trans]
276	    · split_ifs
277	      exact ((if_neg hV).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext ..
278	
279	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
280	/-- If `f : 𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c` is a natural transformation, then there is a morphism
281	`𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c` defined as the morphism from colimit to cocone at `c`.
282	-/
283	def fromStalk {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c) : 𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c :=
284	  let χ : Cocone ((OpenNhds.inclusion p₀).op ⋙ 𝓕) :=
285	    Cocone.mk c <|
286	      { app := fun U => f.app ((OpenNhds.inclusion p₀).op.obj U) ≫ eqToHom (if_pos U.unop.2)
287	        naturality := fun U V inc => by
288	          dsimp only [Functor.const_obj_map, Functor.const_obj_obj, Functor.comp_map,
289	            Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, skyscraperPresheaf_obj]
290	          rw [Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc, comp_eqToHom_iff, Category.assoc,
291	            eqToHom_trans, f.naturality, skyscraperPresheaf_map]
292	          have hV : p₀ ∈ (OpenNhds.inclusion p₀).obj V.unop := V.unop.2
293	          simp only [dif_pos hV] }
294	  colimit.desc _ χ
295	
296	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
297	lemma germ_fromStalk {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c) (U) (hU) :
298	    𝓕.germ U p₀ hU ≫ fromStalk p₀ f = f.app (op U) ≫ eqToHom (if_pos hU) :=
299	  colimit.ι_desc _ _
300	
301	theorem to_skyscraper_fromStalk {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c) :
302	    toSkyscraperPresheaf p₀ (fromStalk _ f) = f := by
303	  apply NatTrans.ext
304	  ext U
305	  dsimp
306	  split_ifs with h
307	  · simp
308	  · exact ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext ..
309	
310	theorem fromStalk_to_skyscraper {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c) :
311	    fromStalk p₀ (toSkyscraperPresheaf _ f) = f := by
312	  refine 𝓕.stalk_hom_ext fun U hxU ↦ ?_
313	  rw [germ_fromStalk, toSkyscraperPresheaf_app, dif_pos hxU, Category.assoc, Category.assoc,
314	    eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id, Presheaf.germ]
315	
316	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
317	/-- The unit in `Presheaf.stalkFunctor ⊣ skyscraperPresheafFunctor`
318	-/
319	@[simps]
320	protected def unit :
321	    𝟭 (Presheaf C X) ⟶ Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀ ⋙ skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀ where
322	  app _ := toSkyscraperPresheaf _ <| 𝟙 _
323	  naturality 𝓕 𝓖 f := by
324	    ext U; dsimp
325	    split_ifs with h
326	    · simp only [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl,
327	        Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_assoc, Presheaf.stalkFunctor_obj]
328	    · apply ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
329	
330	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
331	/-- The counit in `Presheaf.stalkFunctor ⊣ skyscraperPresheafFunctor`
332	-/
333	@[simps]
334	protected def counit :
335	    skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀ ⋙ (Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀ : Presheaf C X ⥤ C) ⟶ 𝟭 C where
336	  app c := (skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes p₀ c specializes_rfl).hom
337	  naturality x y f := TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext _ fun U hxU ↦ by simp [hxU]
338	
339	end StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs
340	
341	section
342	
343	open StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs
344	
345	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
346	/-- `skyscraperPresheafFunctor` is the right adjoint of `Presheaf.stalkFunctor`
347	-/
348	def skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction [HasColimits C] :
349	    (Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀ : Presheaf C X ⥤ C) ⊣ skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀ where
350	  unit := StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs.unit _
351	  counit := StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs.counit _
352	  left_triangle_components X := by
353	    dsimp [Presheaf.stalkFunctor, toSkyscraperPresheaf]
354	    ext
355	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, ι_colimMap_assoc, skyscraperPresheaf_obj,
356	      Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Category.comp_id]
357	    split_ifs with h
358	    · simp [skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes]
359	      rfl
360	    · simp only [skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes, colimit.isoColimitCocone_ι_hom,
361	        skyscraperPresheafCoconeOfSpecializes_pt, skyscraperPresheafCoconeOfSpecializes_ι_app,
362	        Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, skyscraperPresheaf_obj, Functor.const_obj_obj]
363	      rw [comp_eqToHom_iff]
364	      apply ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
365	  right_triangle_components Y := by
366	    ext
367	    simp only [skyscraperPresheafFunctor_obj, Functor.id_obj, skyscraperPresheaf_obj,
368	      Presheaf.stalkFunctor_obj, unit_app, counit_app,
369	      skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes, skyscraperPresheafFunctor_map, Presheaf.comp_app,
370	      toSkyscraperPresheaf_app, Category.id_comp, SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_app]
371	    split_ifs with h
372	    · simp [Presheaf.germ]
373	      rfl
374	    · simp
375	      rfl

377	instance [HasColimits C] : (skyscraperPresheafFunctor p₀ : C ⥤ Presheaf C X).IsRightAdjoint :=
378	  (skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction _).isRightAdjoint
379	
380	instance [HasColimits C] : (Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀).IsLeftAdjoint :=
381	  -- Use a classical instance instead of the one from `variable`s
382	  have : ∀ U : Opens X, Decidable (p₀ ∈ U) := fun _ ↦ Classical.dec _
383	  (skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction _).isLeftAdjoint
384	
385	/-- Taking stalks of a sheaf is the left adjoint functor to `skyscraperSheafFunctor`
386	-/
387	def stalkSkyscraperSheafAdjunction [HasColimits C] :
388	    Sheaf.forget C X ⋙ Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ p₀ ⊣ skyscraperSheafFunctor p₀ where
389	  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11041): `ext1` is changed to `Sheaf.Hom.ext`,
390	  unit :=
391	    { app := fun 𝓕 => ⟨(StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs.unit p₀).app 𝓕.1⟩
392	      naturality := fun 𝓐 𝓑 f => Sheaf.hom_ext <| by
393	        apply (StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs.unit p₀).naturality }
394	  counit := StalkSkyscraperPresheafAdjunctionAuxs.counit p₀
395	  left_triangle_components X :=
396	    ((skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction p₀).left_triangle_components X.obj)
397	  right_triangle_components _ :=
398	    Sheaf.hom_ext ((skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction p₀).right_triangle_components _)
399	
400	instance [HasColimits C] : (Sheaf.forget C X ⋙ Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀).IsLeftAdjoint :=
401	  have : ∀ U : Opens X, Decidable (p₀ ∈ U) := fun _ ↦ Classical.dec _
402	  (stalkSkyscraperSheafAdjunction p₀).isLeftAdjoint
403	
404	instance [HasColimits C] : (skyscraperSheafFunctor p₀ : C ⥤ Sheaf C X).IsRightAdjoint :=
405	  (stalkSkyscraperSheafAdjunction _).isRightAdjoint
406	
407	/-- Taking stalks is the left adjoint of `skyscraperSheafFunctor ⋙ Sheaf.forget`. Useful
408	only when the fact that `skyscraperPresheafFunctor` factors through `Sheaf C X` is relevant. -/
409	noncomputable def skyscraperSheafForgetAdjunction [HasColimits C] :
410	    Presheaf.stalkFunctor C p₀ ⊣ skyscraperSheafFunctor p₀ ⋙ Sheaf.forget C X :=
411	  skyscraperPresheafStalkAdjunction p₀

10	/-!
11	# Presheaves on `PUnit`
12	
13	Presheaves on `PUnit` satisfy sheaf condition iff its value at empty set is a terminal object.
14	-/
15	
16	public section
17	
18	
19	namespace TopCat.Presheaf
20	
21	universe u v w
22	
23	open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits TopCat Opposite
24	
25	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
26	
27	theorem isSheaf_of_isTerminal_of_indiscrete {X : TopCat.{w}} (hind : X.str = ⊤) (F : Presheaf C X)
28	    (it : IsTerminal <| F.obj <| op ⊥) : F.IsSheaf := fun c U s hs => by
29	  have : IndiscreteTopology X := ⟨hind⟩
30	  obtain rfl | hne := eq_or_ne U ⊥
31	  · intro _ _
32	    rw [@existsUnique_iff_exists _ ⟨fun _ _ => _⟩]
33	    · refine ⟨it.from _, fun U hU hs => IsTerminal.hom_ext ?_ _ _⟩
34	      rwa [le_bot_iff.1 hU.le]
35	    · apply it.hom_ext
36	  · convert Presieve.isSheafFor_top (F ⋙ coyoneda.obj (@op C c))
37	    rw [Sieve.arrows_eq_top_iff, ← Sieve.id_mem_iff_eq_top]
38	    have := U.eq_bot_or_top.resolve_left hne
39	    subst this
40	    obtain he | ⟨⟨x⟩⟩ := isEmpty_or_nonempty X
41	    · exact (hne <| SetLike.ext'_iff.2 <| Set.univ_eq_empty_iff.2 he).elim
42	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hm⟩ := hs x _root_.trivial
43	    obtain rfl | rfl := U.eq_bot_or_top
44	    · cases hm
45	    · convert hf
46	
47	theorem isSheaf_iff_isTerminal_of_indiscrete {X : TopCat.{w}} (hind : X.str = ⊤)
48	    (F : Presheaf C X) : F.IsSheaf ↔ Nonempty (IsTerminal <| F.obj <| op ⊥) :=
49	  ⟨fun h => ⟨Sheaf.isTerminalOfEmpty ⟨F, h⟩⟩, fun ⟨it⟩ =>
50	    isSheaf_of_isTerminal_of_indiscrete hind F it⟩
51	
52	theorem isSheaf_on_punit_of_isTerminal (F : Presheaf C (TopCat.of PUnit))
53	    (it : IsTerminal <| F.obj <| op ⊥) : F.IsSheaf :=
54	  isSheaf_of_isTerminal_of_indiscrete (@Subsingleton.elim (TopologicalSpace PUnit) _ _ _) F it
55	
56	theorem isSheaf_on_punit_iff_isTerminal (F : Presheaf C (TopCat.of PUnit)) :
57	    F.IsSheaf ↔ Nonempty (IsTerminal <| F.obj <| op ⊥) :=
58	  ⟨fun h => ⟨Sheaf.isTerminalOfEmpty ⟨F, h⟩⟩, fun ⟨it⟩ => isSheaf_on_punit_of_isTerminal F it⟩
59	
60	end TopCat.Presheaf

100	lemma isConstant_of_isIso_counit_app (F : Sheaf J D) [HasTerminal C]
101	    [IsIso <| (constantSheafAdj J D terminalIsTerminal).counit.app F] : IsConstant J F where
102	  mem_essImage := ⟨_, ⟨asIso <| (constantSheafAdj J D terminalIsTerminal).counit.app F⟩⟩
103	
104	instance [(constantSheaf J D).Faithful] [(constantSheaf J D).Full] (F : Sheaf J D)
105	    [IsConstant J F] {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
106	    IsIso ((constantSheafAdj J D hT).counit.app F) := by

110	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
111	/--
112	If the constant sheaf functor is fully faithful, then a sheaf is constant if and only if the
113	counit of the constant sheaf adjunction applied to it is an isomorphism.
114	-/
115	lemma isConstant_iff_isIso_counit_app [(constantSheaf J D).Faithful] [(constantSheaf J D).Full]
116	    (F : Sheaf J D) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
117	      IsConstant J F ↔ (IsIso <| (constantSheafAdj J D hT).counit.app F) :=
118	  ⟨fun _ ↦ inferInstance, fun _ ↦ ⟨_, ⟨asIso <| (constantSheafAdj J D hT).counit.app F⟩⟩⟩
119	
120	/--
121	A variant of `isConstant_iff_isIso_counit_app` for a general left adjoint to evaluation at a
122	terminal object.
123	-/
124	lemma isConstant_iff_isIso_counit_app' {L : D ⥤ Sheaf J D} {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
125	    (adj : L ⊣ (sheafSections J D).obj ⟨T⟩)
126	    [L.Faithful] [L.Full] (F : Sheaf J D) : IsConstant J F ↔ IsIso (adj.counit.app F) :=
127	  (isConstant_iff_mem_essImage J hT adj F).trans (isIso_counit_app_iff_mem_essImage adj).symm
128	
129	end Sheaf

131	section Equivalence
132	variable {C' : Type*} [Category* C'] (K : GrothendieckTopology C') [HasWeakSheafify K D]
133	variable (G : C ⥤ C') [∀ (X : (C')ᵒᵖ), HasLimitsOfShape (StructuredArrow X G.op) D]
134	  [G.IsDenseSubsite J K] {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) (hT' : IsTerminal (G.obj T))
135	
136	open IsDenseSubsite
137	
138	variable (D) in
139	/--
140	The constant sheaf functor commutes up to isomorphism the equivalence of sheaf categories induced
141	by a dense subsite.
142	-/
143	noncomputable def equivCommuteConstant :
144	    constantSheaf J D ⋙ (sheafEquiv J K G D).functor ≅ constantSheaf K D :=
145	  ((constantSheafAdj J D hT).comp (sheafEquiv J K G D).toAdjunction).leftAdjointUniq
146	    (constantSheafAdj K D hT')
147	
148	variable (D) in
149	/--
150	The constant sheaf functor commutes up to isomorphism the inverse equivalence of sheaf categories
151	induced by a dense subsite.
152	-/
153	noncomputable def equivCommuteConstant' :
154	    constantSheaf J D ≅ constantSheaf K D ⋙ (sheafEquiv J K G D).inverse :=
155	  isoWhiskerLeft (constantSheaf J D) (sheafEquiv J K G D).unitIso ≪≫
156	    isoWhiskerRight (equivCommuteConstant J D K G hT hT') (sheafEquiv J K G D).inverse
157	
158	/- TODO: find suitable assumptions for proving generalizations of `equivCommuteConstant` and
159	`equivCommuteConstant'` above, to commute `constantSheaf` with pullback/pushforward of sheaves. -/

161	include hT hT' in
162	/--
163	The property of a sheaf of being constant is invariant under equivalence of sheaf
164	categories.
165	-/
166	lemma Sheaf.isConstant_iff_of_equivalence (F : Sheaf K D) :
167	    ((sheafEquiv J K G D).inverse.obj F).IsConstant J ↔ IsConstant K F := by
168	  constructor
169	  · exact fun ⟨Y, ⟨i⟩⟩ ↦ ⟨_, ⟨(equivCommuteConstant J D K G hT hT').symm.app _ ≪≫
170	      (sheafEquiv J K G D).functor.mapIso i ≪≫ (sheafEquiv J K G D).counitIso.app _⟩⟩
171	  · exact fun ⟨Y, ⟨i⟩⟩ ↦ ⟨_, ⟨(equivCommuteConstant' J D K G hT hT').app _ ≪≫
172	      (sheafEquiv J K G D).inverse.mapIso i⟩⟩
173	
174	end Equivalence

200	    ((constantCommuteCompose J U).hom.app (F.obj.obj ⟨T⟩)) ≫
201	      ((constantSheafAdj J B hT).counit.app ((sheafCompose J U).obj F)) =
202	        ((sheafCompose J U).map ((constantSheafAdj J D hT).counit.app F)) := by
203	  apply Sheaf.hom_ext
204	  dsimp
205	  rw [constantCommuteCompose_hom_app_hom, assoc, Iso.inv_comp_eq]
206	  apply sheafify_hom_ext _ _ _ ((sheafCompose J U).obj F).property
207	  ext x

213	    [constantSheaf J B |>.Faithful] [constantSheaf J B |>.Full]
214	    [(sheafCompose J U).ReflectsIsomorphisms] [((sheafCompose J U).obj F).IsConstant J]

216	  have : IsIso ((sheafCompose J U).map ((constantSheafAdj J D hT).counit.app F)) := by

224	  exact ⟨U.obj Y, ⟨(fullyFaithfulSheafToPresheaf _ _).preimageIso
225	    (((sheafifyComposeIso J U ((const Cᵒᵖ).obj Y)).symm ≪≫
226	      (presheafToSheaf J B ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B).mapIso (constComp Cᵒᵖ Y U)).symm ≪≫
227	        (sheafToPresheaf _ _).mapIso ((sheafCompose J U).mapIso i))⟩⟩

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2021 Junyan Xu. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Junyan Xu, Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7

9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Pullback
10	
11	/-!
12	# functors between categories of sheaves
13	
14	Show that the pushforward of a sheaf is a sheaf, and define
15	the pushforward functor from the category of C-valued sheaves
16	on X to that of sheaves on Y, given a continuous map between
17	topological spaces X and Y.
18	
19	## Main definitions
20	- `TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward`:
21	    The pushforward functor between sheaf categories over topological spaces.
22	- `TopCat.Sheaf.pullback`: The pullback functor between sheaf categories over topological spaces.
23	- `TopCat.Sheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`:
24	  The adjunction between pullback and pushforward for sheaves on topological spaces.
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	
31	noncomputable section
32	
33	universe w v u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory
36	
37	open CategoryTheory.Limits
38	
39	open TopologicalSpace
40	
41	open scoped AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
44	variable {X Y : TopCat.{w}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
45	variable ⦃ι : Type w⦄ {U : ι → Opens Y}
46	
47	namespace TopCat
48	
49	namespace Sheaf
50	
51	open Presheaf
52	
53	/-- The pushforward of a sheaf (by a continuous map) is a sheaf.
54	-/
55	theorem pushforward_sheaf_of_sheaf {F : X.Presheaf C} (h : F.IsSheaf) : (f _* F).IsSheaf :=
56	  (Opens.map f).op_comp_isSheaf _ _ ⟨_, h⟩
57	
58	variable (C)
59	
60	/-- The pushforward functor.
61	-/
62	def pushforward (f : X ⟶ Y) : X.Sheaf C ⥤ Y.Sheaf C :=
63	  (Opens.map f).sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _
64	
65	lemma pushforward_forget (f : X ⟶ Y) :
66	    pushforward C f ⋙ forget C Y = forget C X ⋙ Presheaf.pushforward C f := rfl
67	
68	/--
69	Pushforward of sheaves is isomorphic (actually definitionally equal) to pushforward of presheaves.
70	-/
71	def pushforwardForgetIso (f : X ⟶ Y) :
72	    pushforward C f ⋙ forget C Y ≅ forget C X ⋙ Presheaf.pushforward C f := Iso.refl _
73	
74	variable {C}
75	
76	@[simp] lemma pushforward_obj_val (f : X ⟶ Y) (F : X.Sheaf C) :
77	    ((pushforward C f).obj F).1 = f _* F.1 := rfl
78	
79	@[simp] lemma pushforward_map (f : X ⟶ Y) {F F' : X.Sheaf C} (α : F ⟶ F') :
80	    ((pushforward C f).map α).1 = (Presheaf.pushforward C f).map α.1 := rfl
81	
82	variable (A : Type*) [Category.{w} A] {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type w}
83	variable [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w} A FA] [HasColimits A]
84	variable [HasLimits A] [PreservesLimits (CategoryTheory.forget A)]
85	variable [PreservesFilteredColimits (CategoryTheory.forget A)]
86	variable [(CategoryTheory.forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
87	
88	/--
89	The pullback functor.
90	-/
91	def pullback (f : X ⟶ Y) : Y.Sheaf A ⥤ X.Sheaf A :=
92	  (Opens.map f).sheafPullback _ _ _
93	
94	/--
95	The pullback of a sheaf is isomorphic (actually definitionally equal) to the sheafification
96	of the pullback as a presheaf.
97	-/
98	def pullbackIso (f : X ⟶ Y) :
99	    pullback A f ≅ forget A Y ⋙ Presheaf.pullback A f ⋙ presheafToSheaf _ _ :=
100	  Functor.sheafPullbackConstruction.sheafPullbackIso _ _ _ _
101	
102	/-- The adjunction between pullback and pushforward for sheaves on topological spaces. -/
103	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f : X ⟶ Y) :
104	    pullback A f ⊣ pushforward A f :=
105	  (Opens.map f).sheafAdjunctionContinuous _ _ _
106	
107	instance : (pullback A f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction A f).isLeftAdjoint
108	instance : (pushforward A f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction A f).isRightAdjoint
109	
110	end Sheaf
111	
112	end TopCat

114	namespace Topology.IsOpenEmbedding
115	
116	open TopCat Sheaf
117	
118	variable (A : Type*) [Category.{w} A]
119	variable {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : IsOpenEmbedding f)
120	
121	/--
122	The "naive" sheaf pullback by an open embedding `f`: on the underlying presheaf, this is just
123	composition by the functor `IsOpenMap.functor f` (sending an open `U` to `f '' U`).
124	-/
125	def sheafPullback : Y.Sheaf A ⥤ X.Sheaf A :=
126	  haveI := Topology.IsOpenEmbedding.functor_isContinuous hf
127	  hf.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _
128	
129	variable {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type w}
130	variable [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w} A FA] [HasColimits A]
131	variable [HasLimits A] [PreservesLimits (CategoryTheory.forget A)]
132	variable [PreservesFilteredColimits (CategoryTheory.forget A)]
133	variable [(CategoryTheory.forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
134	
135	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
136	/--
137	The pullback of a sheaf by an open embedding `f` is isomorphic to its naive pullback
138	`IsOpenEmbedding.sheafPullback`, i.e. to the composition by the functor `IsOpenMap.functor f`.
139	Also, this is an isomorphism of functors.
140	-/
141	def sheafPullbackIso : Sheaf.pullback A f ≅ hf.sheafPullback A := by
142	  refine Sheaf.pullbackIso A f ≪≫ NatIso.ofComponents (fun F ↦ ?_) (fun u ↦ ?_)
143	  · exact (presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↑X) A).mapIso
144	      (hf.isOpenMap.pullbackIso.app _) ≪≫
145	      (fullyFaithfulSheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) A).preimageIso
146	      (isoSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
147	      (TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_of_isOpenEmbedding hf F.2)).symm
148	  · dsimp
149	    rw [← Functor.map_comp_assoc, hf.isOpenMap.pullbackIso.hom.naturality, Sheaf.hom_ext_iff]
150	    simp only [Functor.whiskeringLeft_obj_obj, Functor.whiskeringLeft_obj_map, Functor.map_comp,
151	      isoSheafify_inv, Category.assoc]
152	    rw [ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom, ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom,
153	      ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom, ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom]
154	    dsimp [sheafPullback, Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous, Sheaf.forget]
155	    simp only [sheafifyMap_sheafifyLift, Category.comp_id, sheafifyMap_sheafifyLift_assoc]
156	    rw [CategoryTheory.sheafifyLift_comp]
157	
158	end Topology.IsOpenEmbedding

11	# Skyscraper sheaves
12	
13	Let `Φ` be a point of a site `(C, J)`. In this file, we construct the
14	skyscraper sheaf functor `skyscraperSheafFunctor : A ⥤ Sheaf J A` and
15	show that it is a right adjoint to `Φ.sheafFiber : Sheaf J A ⥤ A`.

17	-/
18

21	universe w v' v u' u

31	/-- Given a point `Φ` on a site `(C, J)`, this is the skyscraper presheaf functor
32	`A ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. -/
33	@[simps!]
34	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafFunctor : A ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A :=
35	  Functor.flip (Φ.fiber.op ⋙ piFunctor.{w}.flip)
36	
37	/-- Given a point `Φ` on a site `(C, J)`, and an object `M` of a category `A`,
38	this is the skyscraper presheaf with value `M`: it sends `X : C` to the
39	product of copies of `M` indexed by `Φ.fiber.obj X`. -/
40	noncomputable abbrev skyscraperPresheaf (M : A) :
41	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A :=
42	  Φ.skyscraperPresheafFunctor.obj M

50	the bijection `(Φ.presheafFiber.obj P ⟶ M) ≃ (P ⟶ Φ.skyscraperPresheaf M)`
51	that is part of the adjunction `skyscraperPresheafAdjunction`. -/
52	@[simps -isSimp apply_app symm_apply]
53	noncomputable def skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv :
54	    (Φ.presheafFiber.obj P ⟶ M) ≃ (P ⟶ Φ.skyscraperPresheaf M) where
55	  toFun f :=
56	    { app X := Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Φ.toPresheafFiber X.unop x P ≫ f)
57	      naturality {X Y} g := by
58	        dsimp
59	        ext y
60	        have := Φ.toPresheafFiber_w g.unop y P
61	        dsimp at this
62	        simp [reassoc_of% this] }
63	  invFun g := Φ.presheafFiberDesc (fun X x ↦ g.app (op X) ≫ Pi.π _ x) (by simp)
64	  left_inv f := by cat_disch
65	  right_inv g := by cat_disch

70	    Φ.toPresheafFiber X x P ≫ Φ.skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv.symm g =
71	      g.app (op X) ≫ Pi.π _ x := by
72	  simp [skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv_symm_apply]

145	    {M : A} {X : C} (R : Sieve X) (hR : R ∈ J X)
146	    (s : Cone (R.arrows.diagram.op ⋙ Φ.skyscraperPresheaf M)) :
147	    ∃ (l : s.pt ⟶ ∏ᶜ fun (_ : Φ.fiber.obj X) ↦ M),
148	      ∀ (j : R.arrows.category) (y : Φ.fiber.obj j.obj.left),
149	        l ≫ Pi.π _ (Φ.fiber.map j.obj.hom y) = s.π.app (op j) ≫ Pi.π _ y := by
150	  suffices ∀ (x : Φ.fiber.obj X), ∃ (l : s.pt ⟶ M),
151	      ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ (g : Y ⟶ X) (hg : R g) (y : Φ.fiber.obj Y) (hy : Φ.fiber.map g y = x),
152	        s.π.app (op (Presieve.categoryMk _ _ hg)) ≫ Pi.π _ y = l by
153	    choose l hl using this
154	    exact ⟨Pi.lift l, fun j y ↦ by simpa using (hl _ j.obj.hom  j.property y rfl).symm⟩
155	  intro x
156	  obtain ⟨Y₁, f₁, hf₁, y₁, hy₁⟩ := Φ.jointly_surjective _ hR x
157	  refine ⟨s.π.app (op (Presieve.categoryMk _ _ hf₁)) ≫ Pi.π _ y₁,
158	    fun Y₂ f₂ hf₂ y₂ hy₂ ↦ ?_⟩

161	        Φ.fiber.map p₁ z = y₁ ∧ Φ.fiber.map p₂ z = y₂ := by
162	    let α₁ : Φ.fiber.elementsMk _ y₁ ⟶ Φ.fiber.elementsMk _ x := ⟨f₁, hy₁⟩
163	    let α₂ : Φ.fiber.elementsMk _ y₂ ⟶ Φ.fiber.elementsMk _ x := ⟨f₂, hy₂⟩
164	    obtain ⟨z, q₁, q₂, fac⟩ := IsCofiltered.cospan α₁ α₂
165	    rw [Subtype.ext_iff] at fac
166	    refine ⟨z.1, q₁.1, q₂.1, z.2, fac, ?_, ?_⟩
167	    all_goals rw [CategoryOfElements.map_snd] -- was `simp`

172	      Presieve.categoryMk _ _ hf₂ :=
173	    ObjectProperty.homMk (Over.homMk p₂)

205	    Sheaf J A :=

213	    Φ.sheafFiber (A := A) ⊣ Φ.skyscraperSheafFunctor :=
214	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv

219	        Φ.skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv_naturality_left_symm f.hom g.hom
220	      homEquiv_naturality_right f g := by
221	        ext : 1
222	        exact Φ.skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv_naturality_right f g }

245	    letI e : (Φ.presheafFiber.obj F.obj ⟶ M) ≃ _ := Φ.skyscraperSheafAdjunction.homEquiv F M
246	    e.symm f = Φ.skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv.symm f.hom := by
247	  simp [skyscraperSheafAdjunction, Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv]

250	    J.W.IsInvertedBy (Φ.presheafFiber (A := A)) := by
251	  intro P₁ P₂ f hf

253	  intro M
254	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff' Φ.skyscraperPresheafHomEquiv.bijective]

261	  W_isInvertedBy_presheafFiber _ _ (W_toSheafify J P)

266	by localization with respect to the class of morphisms `J.W`. -/
267	noncomputable def presheafToSheafCompSheafFiberIso [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
268	    presheafToSheaf J A ⋙ Φ.sheafFiber ≅ Φ.presheafFiber :=
269	  (NatIso.ofComponents

277	    Localization.Lifting (presheafToSheaf J A) J.W

279	  iso := Φ.presheafToSheafCompSheafFiberIso A

282	  have : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{w, w} (Φ.sheafFiber (A := A)) := inferInstance

def isTerminalOfEmpty (F : Sheaf C X) : Limits.IsTerminal (F.obj.obj (op ⊥)) :=
  F.isTerminalOfBotCover ⊥ (fun _ h => h.elim)

/-- A variant of `isTerminalOfEmpty` that is easier to `apply`. -/

    Limits.IsTerminal (F.obj.obj (op U)) := by
  convert F.isTerminalOfEmpty

@[deprecated "Use ObjectProperty.obj" (since := "2026-03-03")]
abbrev Sheaf.val (F : Sheaf J A) : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A := F.obj

@[deprecated "Use ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.property" (since := "2026-03-03")]
lemma Sheaf.cond (F : Sheaf J A) : Presheaf.IsSheaf J F.obj := F.property

@[deprecated (since := "2026-03-03")]
alias Sheaf.Hom.mk := ObjectProperty.homMk

lemma Sheaf.hom_ext_iff {F G : Sheaf J A} {f g : F ⟶ G} :
--

/-- If the empty sieve is a cover of `X`, then `F(X)` is terminal. -/
def Sheaf.isTerminalOfBotCover (F : Sheaf J A) (X : C) (H : ⊥ ∈ J X) :
    IsTerminal (F.1.obj (op X)) := by
  refine @IsTerminal.ofUnique _ _ _ ?_
  intro Y
  choose t h using F.2 Y _ H (by tauto) (by tauto)
  exact ⟨⟨t⟩, fun a => h.2 a (by tauto)⟩

variable (J) in
/-- A terminal object in `A` gives rise to a terminal object in `Sheaf J` -/
@[simps]
def Sheaf.terminal {X : A} (hX : IsTerminal X) : Sheaf J A where
  obj := (CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj X
  property := Presheaf.isSheaf_of_isTerminal J hX

variable (J) in
/-- The constant sheaf of a terminal object is indeed terminal -/
def Sheaf.isTerminalTerminal {X : A} (hX : IsTerminal X) : IsTerminal (Sheaf.terminal J hX) :=
  .ofUniqueHom (⟨(Functor.isTerminalConst _ hX).from ·.obj⟩)
    (by intros; ext; simpa using hX.hom_ext _ _)

@[simp]
lemma Sheaf.isTerminalTerminal_from_hom {X : A} (hX : IsTerminal X) (G : Sheaf J A) :
    ((Sheaf.isTerminalTerminal J hX).from G).hom = (Functor.isTerminalConst _ hX).from G.obj := rfl

/-- If the topology is discrete, any sheaf is terminal. -/

  in-scope lemma now has internal axiom-clean structure). HARD BAR not met
  (≥1 full closure); blueprint-level objective remains open.

  type is `Subsingleton`; `Subsingleton.elim` discharges the existential.
- **Result for empty branch:** RESOLVED axiom-clean (~20 LOC).
- **Approach for non-empty branch:** The sheafification unit
  `η : (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj A → ((constantSheaf J D).obj A).val` should be
  iso on every non-empty open of an irreducible space (intuition: on
  irreducible X, every non-empty open is connected/dense, so the locally-
  constant sheafification coincides with the literal constant presheaf
  pointwise). With `η_V : A → F(V)` iso for non-empty V, lift any `y ∈ F(V)`
  via `η_V⁻¹`, then push back via `η_U` to get a preimage in `F(U)`;
  naturality of `η` makes `η_U(a) |_ V = η_V(a) = y`.
- **Mathlib gap:** No direct lemma `(constantSheaf J D).obj A.val.obj (op V) ≅ A`
  for non-empty V on irreducible X. Closure requires either the plus-
  construction infrastructure (`Sheaf.IsConstant` framework for irreducible
  spaces) or a direct unit-iso-on-nonempty-opens computation.
- **Dead end (do not retry):** `infer_instance`, `aesop`, `simp` — none unfold
  the sheafification automatically; the structural irreducibility-of-X
  argument has no automation handle in Mathlib `b80f227`.

    `skyscraperPresheaf_eq_pushforward P A` lifts to a sheaf-level **equality**
    `skyscraperSheaf P A = (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward _ const).obj (skyscraperSheaf PUnit.unit A)`
    via `ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.ext` (since sheaves are presheaves +
    Prop-level `IsSheaf`; the IsSheaf proofs are subsingleton-equal).

  - **Compose:** `(eqToIso outer).trans ((Sheaf.pushforward).mapIso inner)`.
- **Result for outer:** RESOLVED axiom-clean (~10 LOC inside proof body).
- **Approach for inner:** Forward map via `(constantSheafAdj J_punit ModuleCat hT⊤).homEquiv.symm`
  applied to `eqToHom hval.symm` where `hval : skyscraperSheaf PUnit.unit A.val.obj (op ⊤) = A`
  is discharged by `simp [skyscraperPresheaf]` (since `PUnit.unit ∈ ⊤`).

For `constant_of_irreducible` non-empty branch:

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.


## Developer feedback channel (optional)

If during this iteration you notice something that would make Archon
better — a missing capability, redundant functionality, a
prompt instruction that contradicts itself, a tool you wish existed,
new ideas for better efficiency, etc — you may leave a short note
for the developer by appending to this file with a bash heredoc:

    mkdir -p /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/.debug-feedback
    cat >> /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/.debug-feedback/debug_feedback.md <<'EOF'

    ## iter-196 · parallel prover

    <your note here, one concrete observation, under ~200 words>
    EOF

Rules:
- This file is WRITE-ONLY from your perspective. Do NOT read it,
  cat it, grep it, or open it in any tool. It is for the developer.
- Only leave a note if you have something concrete to say. Empty or
  generic feedback ("everything went fine") is noise — skip it.
- One concrete observation per note. Keep it under ~200 words.
- This is optional and does not affect your task. Skip it if nothing
  comes to mind.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
